ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers brewed oolong tea; the less-sugar variant is not separately coded. This is considered a 'closest_parent' match as it represents the base food item. Sugar addition is documented in notes.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of oolong tea (less sugar) is approximately 250 ml, equivalent to 250 g, as commonly served at Singaporean hawker centres and tea stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Oolong Tea (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Oolong Tea (Less Sugar), cr_id D08010077. Sodium field is -1 in the source record, indicating the value was not reported by HPB for this preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for oolong tea with less sugar. GI estimated at 45 (low category) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened tea beverages with moderate sugar content (approximately 3–4 g sugar per 100 ml). The sugar content of 3.17 g/100 g drives a modest glycemic response. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (45 × 6.07 × 2.5) / 100 = 6.8 g, rounded to one decimal place.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (3.17 g/100 g) with negligible dietary fibre (0.03 g/100 g) and sodium not reported; added sugar promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention and endothelial dysfunction relevant to blood pressure management.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request fully unsweetened oolong tea, as even reduced-sugar preparations retain added sugar that can contribute to insulin-mediated sodium retention and impair blood pressure control over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 3.17 g added sugar per 100 g and negligible dietary fibre (0.03 g/100 g); classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) regardless of the reduced-sugar designation, triggering the diabetes hard gate.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this beverage and choose plain unsweetened oolong tea (0 g sugar) instead, as any added sugar in a liquid form raises postprandial blood glucose rapidly with no fibre buffer.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar (3.17 g/100 g) with zero fat and negligible fibre; regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and increased cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit sugar-sweetened tea beverages and opt for unsweetened oolong tea, which provides polyphenol antioxidants without the added sugar load that adversely affects lipid profiles.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar introduces a pro-inflammatory dietary component that may exacerbate systemic inflammation relevant to rheumatoid arthritis, outweighing the trace polyphenol benefit in this sweetened preparation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should switch to unsweetened oolong tea to benefit from its polyphenol content without the pro-inflammatory effect of added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Oolong Tea (Less Sugar) is a freshly brewed Chinese-origin tea beverage widely consumed in Singapore, prepared by steeping oolong tea leaves in hot water and adding a reduced quantity of sugar compared to the standard sweetened version. Oolong tea occupies a flavour and oxidation level between green tea and black tea, producing a light amber liquor with a mildly floral, slightly roasted, and subtly sweet taste. The less-sugar variant retains 3.17 g of sugar per 100 ml, making it a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) despite the reduced-sugar designation. Per 100 g, it provides 24 kcal, 6.07 g carbohydrate, 0 g protein, and 0 g fat. Protein and fat are naturally zero as this is a brewed tea beverage with no milk or protein additives. Dietary fibre is negligible at 0.03 g/100 g. Sodium data was not reported by HPB for this preparation. Calcium is 1.0 mg/100 g and iron is 0.05 mg/100 g, both trace amounts. Clinically, this beverage is classified as an SSB and is not suitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its added sugar content. Unsweetened oolong tea is the preferred alternative for health-conscious consumers. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low category) based on analogous sweetened tea data from the University of Sydney GI Database, with a glycemic load of approximately 6.8 per 250 ml serving.

Teh oolong kurang gula sesuai untuk pesakit kencing manis?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D08010077, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. The exact food entry was matched. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and is documented as such.
notes: ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): protein_g is naturally zero as this is a brewed tea beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero as this is a brewed tea beverage with no fat-containing additives; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D08010077. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by formulation. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (g3): sodium_mg is set to -1 because the HPB Singapore Foodo source record reports sodium as -1.0 for this preparation, indicating the value was not measured or reported; no alternative approved source provides sodium data for this specific less-sugar oolong tea preparation. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these micronutrient and fatty acid values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this entry and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific less-sugar oolong tea preparation as of 2026. GI NOTE: Glycemic index of 45 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sweetened tea beverages with comparable sugar content (~3–4 g/100 ml); no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources. HEALTH SUITABILITY: This beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to the presence of added sugar (3.17 g/100 g) and is not suitable for type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis management. ONTOLOGY: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers brewed oolong tea; no separate FoodEx2 code exists for the less-sugar variant, hence 'closest_parent' match type.
